summary_one_line: A parseable name table with >=1 valid NameRecord so names.length>0 and the post-qsort loop iterates at least once.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A name-table header whose magic/structural tags (the 'name' sfnt tag and NameRecord platform/encoding/nameID/language CMP fields) match the values harfbuzz tests at parse time, so all_names is non-empty and at least one entry survives into names.length, making the line-504 loop take its true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed carries literal sfnt-table tags ('typ1', 'name', and GB/G tag bytes) at the table-directory offsets, populating NameRecords so names.length becomes 12 and the loop iterates; the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser never plants those exact structural tags; its name table parses to zero usable records, names.length stays 0, and the loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds where the program compared the input against the expected sfnt tag / NameRecord constants, and substitutes those exact constants into the input — value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact tag bytes, so vpc beats vp on this axis.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same structural name-table fields, but reached incrementally: byte sequences progressively closer to the expected platform/encoding/language constants so that near-matching inputs are retained and refined into a fully-parseable record set with names.length>0.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner accretes the right structural bytes; against plain cmplog the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 versus the loser's 1/10 and hitcount 2, the extra gain coming from CMP_MAP gradient retention of partial matches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partially-correct intermediates are not preserved as corpus and the path to a complete parseable record set is not climbed; cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount only 2.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching the expected name-table constants, letting near-matching intermediates be refined toward names.length>0; plain cmplog lacks this gradient retention, so vpc beats cmplog on this axis.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a loop guarded by this->names.length, requiring at least one surviving name entry to take the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->names.length; i++)

evidence_trail.claim: names.length is populated from parsed NameRecords (all_names) built from the table's nameRecordZ/count.
Pair 1 winner value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile with the delta being the I2S technique.
On Pair 1 the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 while the loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=12  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seed contains literal sfnt structural tags including 'name'.
The winner seed also carries the 'typ1' table tag bytes at the start of the input.
cmplog supplies I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 2 winner value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog with the delta being the value_profile technique.
On Pair 2 the winner has hitcount 12 versus the cmplog loser's hitcount 2.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=12  loser=2

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching via a new CMP_MAP bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The winner resolves the branch and reaches the per-name dedup body (lines 504-513) that the loser never executes.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5251 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 2 --seed 55e02621633263d5`; the I2S-floor signal reports 1 ancestor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 49 of the chain, which under the original canonical set is I2S-exclusive for vpc and corroborates an I2SRandReplace contribution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the name-table tags / NameRecord constants are placed in the seed and value_profile alone (no I2S) resolves the line-504 loop as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing exact constant placement was not the bottleneck.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff evidence shows winner-side tags but does not isolate which CMP constant the I2S substitution actually solved, so the specific NameRecord field gating names.length is inferred rather than directly observed.
